name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: As work continues, I am hopeful that we can find a solution to reopen the government.  The American people, particularly our veterans & troops, deserve better. My office stands ready to assist Alaskans, including those negatively impacted by this unnecessary government shutdown.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: I simply fail to understand the logic behind the Minority Leader’s decision to shut down the govt. Passage of the CR tonight would have continued the essential functions of govt, provided funding for CHIP & given Congress an opportunity to address a long term spending measure.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: The bipartisan bill we considered tonight, which funded the government until February 16th, was voted down not for the items it contained, but for what was not included – a long term agreement on immigration for which we still have weeks to resolve.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Tonight, we saw a manufactured crisis officially turn into a government shutdown – something that was absolutely preventable.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: The bill before us prevents a government shutdown, funds CHIP for six years and delays a number of harmful Obamacare taxes. While a resolution on immigration reform is important, funding the government must take priority.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: While I believe a resolution on immigration reform – one that includes an increase in border security – should occur, keeping the government open and operational must take priority.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: The short-term funding measure currently before us, which I support, contains numerous provisions that enjoy nearly universal backing in Alaska and Congress – including a six year extension of CHIP and delays to Obamacare’s Cadillac and Medical Device taxes.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Yet tonight, in the face of a government shutdown, the Senate Minority Leader is now emphasizing an issue he has rarely focused on – rebuilding our military – while failing to mention his primary reason for shutting down the govt – a lack of an agreement on immigration reform.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: For months, Congress has worked to debate and negotiate a long term spending measure – one that appropriately supports our men and women in uniform, establishes policies that grow our economy, and tackles a series of leading national priorities.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: The only thing worse than another Continuing Resolution – particularly for our military and veterans – is a government shutdown.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Mr. President, the people who sent us here still have big dreams and big hopes. Let’s help those dreams grow and their hopes flourish.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: There is NO doubt of that.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Despite challenges, despite big-govt’s creep into our lives, despite armed EPA agents, we continue to live in greatest country in the world.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: I believe our government should be helping, not hindering, these efforts.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: And I think most of us can agree that we must unleash the country’s enormous economic potential once again.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: We want a government that holds dear what our fathers knew – that all powers are derived from the consent of the governed.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: We want to continue to live in the best country in the world, a country of unlimited opportunity, a country of Alaska-sized dreams.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: We want our children to be safe and secure and we want the same for our neighbor.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: We want to be strong here at home which will help us be respected once again by our allies and feared by our adversaries.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: We all want the very best for the people we represent and the states we speak for.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: let me close with a few words about how I view my mission here. I suspect it doesn’t differ from what most of us here hope to accomplish.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: And finally we must challenge the conventional wisdom that the federal government’s power &  intrusiveness should always be expanding

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Fourth: while I believe in a limited fed. govt, we must recognize where the fed. govt DOES have responsibilities -- like protecting our Vets

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Third: we must get back to the rule of law.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Second, we need more, not less, access to our federal lands. As Americans, these are OUR lands – not the @Interior or @BLMNational

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Let me suggest a few ideas: First – We MUST stop delaying economic projects that benefit our citizens.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: We must work to once again unleash the great potential that is Alaska and America & we must work to reinvigorate faith in the American dream

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Congress has a job to do. We must work to address the anxiety and frustration of the people we serve.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: What the American people are telling us, is that they want a federal government that helps ignite their hope, not smother it.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Our citizens are telling us that their government has gone well beyond deriving its powers from the consent of the governed.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: And judging from what Americans are telling us, the reach of the federal government has exceeded its limits.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Since the founding of this country, we’ve had important debates right here on this floor about the role of the fed. government in our lives.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Yes, we have major challenges like all states. But in Alaska, we still have hope, we still dream big dreams. TAPS helps fuel these dreams.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: I’m standing in support of what has defined this country for centuries: The idea of the American dream.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: In supporting Keystone, I’m  standing for a larger principle. The ideal that the fed. govt should be a partner in progress. Not an obstacle.

name: Sen. Dan Sullivan
tweet: Today I stand in support of Keystone. As an Alaskan I feel it’s important to talk about this bill & the importance of energy infrastructure
